channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Trump calls US team ahead of hockey final against Canada. Fans say they aren't there for politics
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Fans waving and wearing flags streamed into the TD Garden on Thursday night for the championship game of the 4 Nations Face-Off, a matchup between the United States and Canada that has taken on collateral importance in the wake of cross-border political animosity.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas is the latest school to cancel its spring game, a college tradition that appears to be fading
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — College football's annual rite of spring seems to be withering. Texas became the latest major program Thursday to ditch or change its annual spring scrimmage that has long been a staple for fans eager to see rising stars and get a peek into the upcoming season.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Milwaukee's Bobby Portis suspended after he mistakenly took painkiller Tramadol rather than Toradol
item.description: The NBA suspended Milwaukee forward Bobby Portis Jr. for 25 games without pay on Thursday because he tested positive for the painkiller Tramadol, a violation of the league's anti-drug program.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe, an 8-year NFL veteran who played his entire career with the Minnesota Vikings, took to the podium to voice his opposition to the plaque design.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Tiger Woods joins another White House meeting as PGA Tour moves closer to Saudi deal
item.description: Tiger Woods joined PGA Tour Commissioner Jay Monahan and player director Adam Scott in a second White House meeting Thursday with President Donald Trump, another sign the sport is moving rapidly toward ending the division brought on by Saudi-funded LIV Golf.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ja Morant has no comment on report that says group targeting pro athletes burglarized his home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two-time NBA All-Star Ja Morant is not talking about a published report that says he is the Memphis player whose home was burglarized by the seven men from Chile charged in federal court with orchestrating break-ins at the homes of prominent athletes around the country.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mets' Juan Soto gifts teammate car for giving up jersey number
item.description: New York Mets superstar outfielder Juan Soto gave Brett Baty a new car after Baty gave up his No. 22 for Soto after the outfielder signed a huge contract with the Mets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami quarterback Carson Beck's luxury cars stolen in Florida: report
item.description: Miami Hurricanes quarterback Carson Beck had two luxury vehicles stolen overnight in Florida, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers advance trans athlete ban bill as state faces investigation for defying Trump order
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers passed a bill that would prevent trans athletes from playing in women's and girls sports after the state refused to comply with Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MLB sees very first challenge using Automatic Balls-Strikes system in spring training
item.description: For the first time in MLB history, a pitch was challenged using the new Automated Ball-Strikes system — and the call on the field was overturned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No. 22 for a new SUV as Juan Soto gives Mets 3B Brett Baty a Tahoe for switching numbers
item.description: PORT ST. LUCIE, Fla. (AP) — Juan Soto got his desired No. 22 jersey with the New York Mets after his record $765 million contract this offseason. Brett Baty, who wore that number the past three seasons, got a brand new SUV in spring training.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama likely to miss rest of season
item.description: Team officials say Wemby's condition — deep vein thrombosis — was diagnosed this week after he returned from the All-Star Game.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Ex-Spain soccer boss Rubiales found guilty of sexual assault and fined for World Cup kiss
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Former Spanish soccer boss Luis Rubiales was found guilty Thursday of sexually assaulting player Jenni Hermoso for kissing her after the 2023 Women’s World Cup final, an incident that overshadowed Spain’s first world title in women’s soccer and sparked debate about sexism in the sport.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 1 Auburn overcomes a rough offensive performance to beat Arkansas 67-60
item.description: AUBURN, Ala. (AP) — Johni Broome had 16 points and 13 rebounds and No. 1 Auburn overcame a rough offensive performance to beat Arkansas 67-60 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lions' Jameson Williams won't be disciplined by NFL after facing arrest in gun-related incident: report
item.description: Detroit Lions wide receiver Jameson Williams will not face discipline by the NFL regarding an October 2024 incident in which he faced arrest on a gun charge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Central Girls Wrestling reached the pinnacle of Colorado Wrestling
item.description:
Central Girls Wrestling, aka D51 Phoenix, featuring girls from all four D51 high schools, has done it.
They are the cream of the crop.
The top of the heap.
The best in the state.
They are the undisputed 5A State Champions.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WGN 9

item.title: Researchers confirm Hall of Famer Bobby Hull had CTE when he died 2 years ago
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Bobby Hull, the Hall of Fame forward and two-time NHL MVP who helped the Chicago Blackhawks win the Stanley Cup in 1961, had chronic traumatic encephalopathy when he died two years ago, his widow said in a statement released on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: There is a ‘renaissance’ in San Francisco, NBA commissioner says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- For the past few days, San Francisco was the center of the basketball universe. It was the first time in 25 years NBA All-Star weekend was in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Cubs manager Craig Counsell goes 1-on-1 with GN Sports from spring training
item.description: MESA, Ariz. — All baseball seasons are a roller-coaster ride to some degree, but Craig Counsell's first season as manager of the Cubs was especially up and down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Angels star Mike Trout moving from center to right field in hopes of staying more healthy
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Los Angeles Angels oft-injured star Mike Trout is moving from center to right field in hopes of better preserving his health. The three-time AL MVP played in 82 or fewer games in three of the past four seasons while dealing with an assortment of injuries.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: College student Jaren Barajas beats Damian Lillard in 3-point shooting contest, wins $100K
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jaren Barajas shoots on his backyard hoop maybe once a month these days. He's a busy 18-year-old college student now, so basketball has taken a backburner to academics.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Padres baseball season in San Diego kicks off March 27 with opening series, East Village Block Party
item.description: The Padres are gearing up for the start of baseball season in San Diego and fans can get excited with the free East Village Block Party that takes place during opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Liverpool continues Premier League title charge and Man United's season keeps getting worse
item.description: Liverpool continued its charge toward the Premier League title on Sunday with a 2-1 win against Wolverhampton. For Manchester United, it just keeps getting worse after a 1-0 loss at Tottenham.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: Montgomery 58, Victory Christian Academy 44
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in Chula Vista on Tuesday night with Montgomery defeating Victory Christian Academy 58-44. Aztecs were lead by Senior JJ Sanchez. Montgomery was down at half and got going in the 2nd half for the double digit win.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Avid volunteer enjoys first Genesis Invitational
item.description: Jenny Liu has volunteered at Torrey Pines for 24 years. She’s seen a lot in her time, but this week it was her first experience with the Genesis Invitational.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Suzuki and Counsell are working on their communication in spring training with the Cubs
item.description: MESA, Ariz. (AP) — Seiya Suzuki is working on his communication with Craig Counsell, and the manager says it's his fault. Suzuki joined the rest of the Chicago Cubs for their first full-squad workout of spring training on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
